LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE. THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT ANNIE NGO'S MOTION FOR ORDER SETTING ASIDE AND VACATING DEFAULT AND GRANTING DEFENDANT LEAVE TO DEFEND IS TRANSFERRED TO DEPT. 501, THE REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING COURT, TO BE HEARD ON MAY 15, 2014 AT 9:30 AM. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR# 5976.

UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, DEFENDANT ANNIE NGO'S MOTION FOR ORDER SETTING ASIDE AND VACATING DEFAULT AND GRANTING DEFENDANT LEAVE TO DEFEND IS DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILS TO SUSTAIN BURDEN UNDER CCP 473.5 AND CCP 473(B). PROOF OF SERVICE ON FILE (BY THE REGISTERED PROCESS SERVER) IS ENTITLED TO REBUTTABLE PRESUMPTION THAT SERVICE WAS PROPER. DEFENDANT FAILS TO ADDRESS IN HER DECLARATION (1) SERVICE ATTEMPTS FROM 11/20-12/8 AND AVOIDANCE THEREOF; (2) WHETHER A PERSON FITTING DESCRIPTION IN THE PROOF OF SERVICE LIVES ON THE PREMISES; (3) SERVICE OF SUMMONS AND COMPLAINT BY MAIL; (4) SERVICE OF REQUEST FOR DEFAULT. ORDER IS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE PAUL H. ALVARADO, CLERK: M. OLOPERNES, NOT REPORTED

MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: Defendant's Renewed Motion for Order Setting Aside and Vacating Default and Default Judgment and Motion for Order to Stay Proceedings in Pending Action until Arbitration Completed is continued to 2:00 p.m. on August 1, 2014 to be heard by the Honorable Paul H. Alvarado in Department 501. (CFH/501/rg)

Defendant Annie Ngo'S Motion For Order Setting Aside And Vacating Default And Default Judgment And Granting Defendant Leave To Defend, And For An Order To Stay Proceedings In Pending Action Until Arbitration Completed
Off Calendar Aug-01-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Ross McLauran Madden, Esq, Attorney at Law, (415-567-1212), for Annie Ngo, Defendant and moving party.
Thomas A. Cohen, Esq., Attorney at Law, (415-777-1997), for Plaintiffs and opposing parties.

Hearing is held. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral arguments rules as follows: Defendant Annie Ngo's Renewed Motion for Order Setting Aside and Vacating Default and Default Judgment and Motion for Order to Stay Proceedings in Pending Action until Arbitration Completed is DENIED. Defendant's arguments on stay and arbitration was not ruled upon by Court. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge Paul H. Alvarado, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not reported (D501)

Default Prove-Up; Summary; Declaration
Off Calendar Jul-01-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514, Judge: Harry M. Dorfman, Clerk: Yolanda Mazariegos, CSR: Mitch Lyon, #6848

Thomas A. Cohen, Esq., appearing for Plaintiff MICHELLE and CHARLES MASTIN.
No appearance by defaulted defendant(s) ANNIE D. NGO.
Default of Defendant(s) has been duly entered.
Court ordered Defendants Does 1-50 dismissed without prejudice.
Plaintiff Michelle Mastin sworn and examinded on her own behalf.
After Evidence and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff and against Defaulted Defendant(s) as follows:

Damages: $133,071.00
Pre-Judgment Interest: $0.00
Attorney's Fees: $8,860.95
Costs: $765.70
Total Judgment in the amount of $142,697.65
Judgment signed in open court.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-03-2014 Continued To Aug-13-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order Setting Aside And Vacating Default And Granting Deft Leave To Defend
Off Calendar May-21-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Thomas A. Cohen, Esq. of Law Offices of Thomas A. Cohen, (415-777-1997), for Michelle Mastin, Plaintiff and opposing party.
No appearance by Defendant and moving party.

Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT ANNIE NGO's Motion For Order Setting Aside And Vacating Default And Granting Defendant Leave To Defend is DENIED. Defendant fails to sustain burden under CCP 473.5 and CCP 473(b). Proof of Service on file (by the registered process server) is entitled to rebuttable presumption that service was proper. Defendant fails to address in her declaration (1) service attempts from 11/20-12/8 and avoidance thereof; (2) whether a person fitting description in the proof of service lives on the premises; (3) service of summons and complaint by mail; (4) service of request for default. Order is signed in open court. Judge Paul H. Alvarado, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not reported (D501)

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order Setting Aside And Vacating Default And Granting Deft Leave To Defend

Thomas A Cohen, Esq. (415-777-1997) for plaintiff/opposing party
Ross McLauran Madden, Esq. (415-567-1212) for defendant/moving party

Defendant/moving party orally makes a peremptory challenge per C.C.P. Section 170.6. Court accepts challenge as timely. DEFENDANT ANNIE NGO's Motion For Order Setting Aside And Vacating Default And Granting Leave To Defend is continued to May-21-2014 at 9:30 AM in Dept. 501. Both sides waive notice. Judge: A.
